message:
refactor(testing): introduce LLMIO injection for mockless unit tests

Architectural change that eliminates the need for global environment
variable mocking (CM_REFLECTOR_STUBS) in unit tests by allowing direct
injection of test doubles via explicit LLMIO parameter.

Changes by file:

src/llm.ts:
- Modify runReflector to only check env-based stubs when using the
  DEFAULT_LLM_IO sentinel value
- When explicit LLMIO is injected, tests control responses directly
  via the io object, bypassing the env var machinery entirely
- Maintains backward compatibility: subprocess E2E tests that rely
  on CM_REFLECTOR_STUBS continue to work unchanged

src/orchestrator.ts:
- Add optional `io?: LLMIO` field to ReflectionOptions interface
- Thread the LLMIO parameter through to reflectOnSession calls
- Enables orchestrator-level integration tests with mock LLM IO

src/reflect.ts:
- Accept optional LLMIO parameter in reflectOnSession
- Only check CM_REFLECTOR_STUBS when io is NOT provided (backward
  compat for subprocess E2E tests that can't inject dependencies)
- Thread io parameter through to runReflector calls

test/reflect.test.ts:
- Complete rewrite from serial tests with env var manipulation to
  parallel tests using withLlmShim() helper for LLMIO injection
- Remove beforeEach/afterEach hooks that managed env vars
- Remove test.serial markers - tests can now run in parallel
- Tests use function callbacks to return different responses per
  iteration (supporting multi-pass reflection testing)
- Remove unused __resetReflectorStubsForTest import

Benefits:
- Tests are no longer order-dependent (parallelizable)
- No global state pollution between tests
- Cleaner test code with explicit dependency injection
- Better separation between unit tests (LLMIO injection) and
  subprocess E2E tests (CM_REFLECTOR_STUBS env var)
